name: Salmon Burgers
tagline: Hand-chopped salmon patties with dill and lemon, no fillers
highlight: Salmon-forward burgers — no shellfish, no fillers, all fish

hero.alt: Salmon burger on bun with dill yogurt and arugula
hero.caption: salmon burger — no fillers

healthFacts: Hand-chopping retains chunky texture — frozen-puck salmon burgers can't compete.
Yogurt-dill sauce keeps the bun bright instead of mayo-heavy.

steps.title: Hand-chop the salmon
steps.detail: Slice salmon into 1/4-inch dice; chop further to a coarse mince. Don't use a food processor — it pastes the fish.
steps.tip: Mix textures: half mince, half larger chunks for bite.

steps.title: Mix and form
steps.detail: Fold salmon with Dijon, lemon zest, half the dill, salt, pepper. Form into 4 patties; chill 10 min.
steps.tip: Chill firms the patties so they hold up in the pan.

steps.title: Whisk yogurt sauce
steps.detail: Whisk yogurt with grated garlic, remaining dill, lemon juice, salt.
steps.tip: A spoon of capers minced into the sauce is dangerously good.

steps.title: Sear and build
steps.detail: Heat olive oil over medium-high. Sear patties 3 min per side. Build on toasted buns with yogurt sauce and arugula.
steps.tip: Don't flip more than once — salmon falls apart with extra handling.
